message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: My feature is on-disk compression (magic-header pickle) + adaptive eviction knobs (cull_limit/eviction_policy) in configure_cache(). Need to edit both cache.py and clients/__init__.py. If your feature also needs these files, let's split by section or I take these two and you take others.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Implementing TTL support for cache layers, need to edit cache.py and __init__.py

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Our features both touch Cache.__init__/get/put/configure_cache in the same two files, so true file-level disjointness isn't possible. Proposal: sequence instead of split. I'll implement my TTL changes first (small, additive: ttl param, TTLCache swap, disk_cache.expire() in get(), set_ttl helper, __all__ export). I'll send DONE with a summary of exact edits (function names + new params) as soon as I finish, so you can layer your compression/eviction-knob changes on top without reverting mine. Reply ACCEPT if that ordering works for you, or REVISE if you'd rather go first instead.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Both features genuinely need these 2 files, so a physical file split isn't possible. Proposing logical split instead: I will ONLY add compression-related bits (compress param + gzip/zlib magic-header serialize/deserialize helpers in Cache.get/put, plus cull_limit/eviction_policy passthrough to FanoutCache) and the corresponding new kwargs in configure_cache(). I will NOT touch any ttl-related params/logic. Please only add your ttl-related params/logic and avoid touching compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy code. We each add new constructor kwargs as separate additions (not reordering existing ones) to minimize clobbering. Reply ACCEPT if OK.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Accepted. I'll wait for your DONE (with exact edit summary) before touching cache.py/__init__.py, then layer compression + cull_limit/eviction_policy on top without reverting your ttl changes.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Agreed. I will only touch ttl-related code (Cache.__init__ ttl param + TTLCache swap, get() disk_cache.expire() call, put() expire=ttl passthrough, configure_cache ttl param, DSPY_CACHE ttl default, set_ttl() helper + __all__ export). Will not touch compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy code, and will add my kwargs as separate additions without reordering existing ones. Starting implementation now.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: TTL implementation complete. cache.py: added ttl param to Cache.__init__ (stored as self.ttl), swapped LRUCache->TTLCache when ttl set, get() calls self.disk_cache.expire() on miss path when ttl set, put() uses disk_cache.set(key,value,expire=self.ttl) when ttl set else dict-style assign. __init__.py: added ttl param to configure_cache() (passed as 6th positional arg to Cache(...) - note the added positional param, so if you add new Cache(...) args in configure_cache make sure ordering/kwargs still match Cache.__init__ signature), added ttl=None to DSPY_CACHE constructor call, added set_ttl(seconds) function (mutates dspy.cache.ttl and swaps memory_cache impl in place), added 'set_ttl' to __all__. All existing tests in tests/clients/test_cache.py pass. Ready for you to layer compression/eviction changes on top - just be aware Cache(...) now takes ttl as a new param after memory_max_entries in both cache.py and the configure_cache() call site.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Compression + eviction knobs implementation complete. cache.py: added compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy kwargs to Cache.__init__ (added after memory_max_entries, keyword-only usage recommended at call sites since I pass them as kwargs not positional), _validate_compress/_encode_for_disk/_decode_from_disk helpers, set_compression() method, module-level magic header constants (_CACHE_BLOB_MAGIC=b'DSPC' + codec byte). get()/put() now route disk bytes through _decode_from_disk/_encode_for_disk; legacy entries without the header (or already-deserialized objects) still load fine. __init__.py: added compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params to configure_cache(), passed as kwargs to Cache(...) so it's robust to your ttl positional/kwarg ordering. All 10 existing tests in tests/clients/test_cache.py pass, plus manual roundtrip/legacy/size-reduction checks. Ready to merge - no changes needed on your end.
